editorial.title: S. Korea can no longer afford to delay nuclear submarine ambitions
editorial.body: [ED] S. Korea can no longer afford to delay nuclear submarine ambitions The Korea Times
editorial.pullQuote: S. Korea can no longer afford to delay nuclear submarine ambitions
summary: South Korea must accelerate its nuclear submarine development program.
Delays risk falling behind regional security threats and strategic competitors.
The nation can no longer postpone this critical military capability.
editorial.stance: South Korea should prioritize nuclear submarine development as essential to regional security.
editorial.titleTr: 한국, 핵잠수함 추진 더 이상 미룰 수 없어
summaryTr: 한국은 핵잠수함 개발 프로그램을 가속화해야 한다.
지연은 지역 안보 위협과 전략적 경쟁국들에 뒤처질 위험이 있다.
이 국가는 더 이상 이 중요한 군사 능력을 미룰 수 없다.

editorial.title: 국민의힘, 지도부 교체와 통합 외에 답 있나
editorial.body: (사설) 국민의힘이 지방선거와 재보궐 선거에서 패배한 원인은 불법계엄과 탄핵 반성 부족, 지도부 무능, 당내 분열에 있다. 오세훈 승리 등 일부 선전에도 지도부 책임론이 부각되며, 국민의힘 미래는 지도부 교체와 당내 통합, 혁신에 달려 있다. |
summary: 국민의힘, 지방선거 패배 원인은 불법계엄과 지도부 무능.
당내 분열과 탄핵 반성 부족이 선거 참패를 초래했다.
지도부 교체와 당내 통합만이 살길이다.
editorial.pullQuote: 국민의힘 미래는 지도부 교체와 당내 통합, 혁신에 달려 있다.
editorial.stance: 보수진영의 재도약을 위해 지도부 책임론을 강하게 제기하며 구조적 혁신을 촉구한다.
editorial.titleTr: Power of the People: Is There Any Answer Beyond Leadership Change and Unity?
summaryTr: The People Power Party attributes its defeat in local elections to the illegal martial law and incompetent leadership.
Internal party divisions and insufficient reflection on the impeachment led to the electoral debacle.
Leadership replacement and party unity are the only way forward.
editorial.bodyTr: The Democratic People's Power Party's defeat in the local elections and by-elections stems from the illegal martial law declaration, insufficient reflection on impeachment, incompetent leadership, and internal party divisions. Despite some propaganda victories such as Oh Se-hoon's win, calls for leadership accountability have become more prominent. The future of the Democratic People's Power Party depends on replacing its leadership, achieving party unity, and pursuing reform.

editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Election Results Reflect Public Demand for President to Reconsider His Direction and Rhetoric
summaryTr: The June 3 local elections sent a warning to the Lee Jae-myung administration against autocratic governance.
The Democratic Party won 12 of 16 races but received mixed signals from the public.
An expression that the sitting president needs to reconsider his direction and rhetoric after two years in office.
editorial.bodyTr: The June 3 local elections sent a dual message to President Lee Jae-myung and the Democratic Party as they approach their second year in office: support for state affairs alongside a warning against arbitrary governance. Though the Democratic Party secured victories in 12 of 16 provincial governorships, the will of the people also contained a clear message of caution.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: The June 3 Public Verdict Appears to Have Rendered Judgment on Rep. Jang Dong-hyuk
summaryTr: Park Jin-suk, the representative of the People Power Party, expressed his refusal to resign by characterizing the party's crushing defeat in the local elections as 'a spark of hope.'
Despite holding Seoul, the party suffered widespread losses in races for provincial governors and other major local offices.
The June 3 public sentiment is being interpreted as a verdict against the current leadership.
editorials.bodyTr: Representative Jang Dong-hyeok of the People Power Party seems to have been judged by the public sentiment of June 3rd. In response to the results of the local elections on the 4th, Jang said the party kept the embers of hope alive. By denying the electoral defeat and hinting that he would not step down from his position as party representative, Jang sent a signal of his intentions. While the People Power Party held onto Seoul in this local election, the metropolitan offices...

editorials.title: [사설] 무능·부패 ‘가족 회사’ 선관위, 수사받고 해체 수준 개혁을
editorials.body: 무능·부패 가족 회사 선관위, 수사받고 해체 수준 개혁을 이재명 대통령이 초유의 투표용지 부족 사태에 대해 매우 큰 유감을 표한다며 명확하게 책임을 묻겠다고 했다. 송파구 공무원은 어떻게 이런 사태가 벌어지도록 송파구 선관위에선 직원이 한 명도 안 올 수가 있느

summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung vows to hold someone accountable for the ballot shortage crisis
He criticizes the election commission as incompetent and corrupt, urging reforms at the level of dismantling the organization
Poor management at the Songpa-gu election commission, including vacant staff positions, cited as the cause of the crisis

editorial.title: [사설] 비밀 유출 논란 자초한 국방장관 발언
editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 안규백 국방부 장관이 지난달 31일(현지시간) 제23차 아시아 안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)가 열리고 있는 싱가포르 샹그릴라 호텔에서 취재진과 만나 현안에 대해 설명하고 있다. [연합뉴스] 전시작전통제권(전작권) 전환을 위한 한·미 양국의 평가 수준을 공개한 안규백 국방부 장관의 발언이 비밀 유출 논란에 휩싸였다. 안 장관은 지난달 31일 싱가포르 출장 중 “한·미 양국은 2020년에 전작권 전환 조건의 94%가 이미 충족됐다고 합의한 것을 비롯해 우리의 능력에 대해 충분히 설명했다”고 밝혔다. 이날 아시아안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)에 참석한 미국 의원들을 만나 한국군의 전작권 행사 능력을 설명하고 협조를 요청했다는 것이다. 한·미는 조건이 갖춰지면 전작권을 한국군에 이양하기로 하고, 구체적인 매뉴얼을 만들어 평가를 진행 중이다. 하지만 구체적인 평가 항목이나 이행 수준은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 간주하고 있다. 자칫 한·미 연합 전력 수준과 방어 태세가 북한 등에 공개될 것을 우려해서다. 그런데 국방부 수장이 나서 양국 군 당국이 공개를 꺼려 온 94%라는 구체적인 수치를 언급한 것이다. 논란이 일자 국방부는 “조속한 전작권 회복과 관련한 국민의 알권리 보장과 이해를 돕기 위해 개략적인 수치를 제시한 것”이라며 “구체적인 내용을 적시한 게 아니어서 한·미 연합 비밀에 해당하지 않는다”고 해명했다. 하지만 국방부는 지난해 전작권 전환 평가 결과에 대한 국회의 자료 제출 요구에 “(전작권과 관련한) 평가 항목 및 결과 등 구체적인 사항은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 답변이 제한된다”며 비공개 입장을 고수한 바 있다. 전작권 평가 추정이 가능한 자료를 공개하지 않겠다는 뜻이다. 군 당국이 지금까지 7년 넘게 국회에 관련 내용을 설명하지 않은 것은 사안의 민감성을 방증한다. 미국은 지난 3월 정동영 통일부 장관의 북한 구성시 우라늄 농축시설 존재 언급을 이유로 아직까지 북핵 동향 관련 정보 제공을 중단하고 있다. 한·미 동맹에 균열 징후가 이어지고 있는 만큼 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다. 한·미는 어제부터 1박2일 동안 한국군의 핵추진잠수함 보유와 우라늄 농축 등과 관련한 협의를 시작했다. 현 정부가 역점을 두고 있는 전작권 환수나 핵추진잠수함 보유 등은 결국 한·미 동맹의 튼튼한 신뢰가 기반이 돼야 순조롭게 진행될 수 있다는 점을 분명히 인식할 필요가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다.
summary: 국방장관이 전작권 전환 평가 수준 94%를 공개해 비밀 유출 논란 초래했다.
군 당국은 한·미 연합 비밀이라며 국회에 자료 제출을 거부해왔다.
한·미 동맹 신뢰가 기반되어야 전작권 환수 등이 순조롭게 진행된다.
editorial.stance: 정부는 민감한 안보 사안에서 일관성 있고 신중한 정보 관리로 한·미 신뢰를 지켜야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Defense Minister's Remarks Invite Classified Information Leak Controversy
summaryTr: The defense minister disclosed a 94% readiness assessment level for the transition of wartime operational control, triggering controversy over secret information leaks.
Military authorities have refused to submit documents to the National Assembly, citing the information as a South Korea-U.S. alliance secret.
The smooth progress of regaining wartime operational control depends on building trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.
editorial.bodyTr:
Defense Minister An Kyou-paik has become embroiled in a controversy over leaked classified information after publicly disclosing the assessment level between South Korea and the United States regarding the transfer of operational control. During a business trip to Singapore last month, Minister An stated, "South Korea and the United States agreed that 94 percent of the conditions for the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON) had already been met as of 2020, and we have sufficiently explained our capabilities." He reportedly met with U.S. lawmakers attending the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore to brief them on the South Korean military's ability to exercise operational control and to request cooperation.

Seoul and Washington have agreed to transfer operational control to the South Korean military once conditions are met, and are currently conducting assessments based on detailed manuals. However, the specific evaluation criteria and implementation levels are classified as a South Korea-U.S. joint military secret due to concerns that operational capability levels and defense postures could be exposed to North Korea and other adversaries.

Yet the head of the Defense Ministry publicly mentioned the specific figure of 94 percent, which military authorities on both sides have been reluctant to disclose. When controversy ensued, the Defense Ministry explained that it had presented only a rough figure to guarantee public awareness and understanding regarding the early recovery of operational control, and that it did not mention specific details and therefore did not constitute a breach of South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. However, the ministry previously maintained a position of non-disclosure when rejecting the National Assembly's request for materials regarding last year's OPCON transfer assessment results, stating that responses were restricted as evaluation criteria and results constituted South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. This amounts to refusing to disclose information from which OPCON assessments could be inferred.

The military authorities' refusal to explain related matters to the National Assembly for over seven years demonstrates the sensitivity of the issue. The United States has suspended the provision of information related to North Korean nuclear developments since March, citing Unification Minister Chung Dong-young's remarks about the existence of uranium enrichment facilities in a unified Korean peninsula. Given the continuing signs of fissures in the South Korea-U.S. alliance, responsible government officials must exercise caution in every word they speak. Starting yesterday, South Korea and the United States began one-and-a-half-day consultations regarding the South Korean military's acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines and uranium enrichment. It must be clearly recognized that priority initiatives of the current government—such as the recovery of operational control and the acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines—can only proceed smoothly when built on a strong foundation of trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 국방장관의 비밀 유출을 비판하며 한·미 동맹의 신뢰와 안보 체계 유지를 강조하는 관료적·보수적 입장으로, 정부 신뢰성과 한·미 연합 기밀 유지를 핵심 프레임으로 삼음

editorial.title: Mandate without excuses
editorial.body: In local elections, voters granted power but demand results, restraint, reform Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages, granting power today while billing governments later. South Korea’s June 3 local elections have left little doubt about who now owns the country's political future. One year after taking office, President Lee Jae Myung's administration has extended its reach across much of the country, with the ruling Democratic Party of Korea capturing 12 of 16 major mayoral and gubernatorial posts. Having already secured the presidency and the National Assembly, it now commands most of the nation’s local political machinery, completing an extraordinary concentration of political power. Yet the election results were not quite the blank check they may appear to be. The main opposition People Power Party retained the Seoul mayoralty, with Oh Se-hoon winning a historic fifth term, and held on to several conservative strongholds. In concurrent parliamentary by-elections, the ruling party surrendered four seats it had previously controlled. Voters strengthened the government, but also signaled that dominance is not consensus. The turnout itself carried a message. At 61.0 percent, participation reached its highest level in a local election since 1995. Such engagement is usually hailed as democratic vitality. Yet the forces driving voters to the polls were less about local governance than national confrontation. The ruling party framed the election as a final judgment on the remnants of the period under former President Yoon Suk Yeol, while conservatives mobilized against the prospect of one-party dominance. The result was a highly energized electorate, but not necessarily a more deliberative one. Indeed, some of the campaign’s weaknesses were hard to miss. Election-law violations rose sharply. Hundreds of candidates ran unopposed in regional strongholds, leaving millions of voters without meaningful choices. What should have been a contest over regional development became another chapter in national political warfare. That failure belongs to both parties. The ruling camp spent much of the campaign focused on settling historical and judicial scores. The opposition remained trapped in arguments about its own past, unable to persuade voters that it had learned from the collapse of the Yoon presidency. Neither side devoted sufficient attention to questions that matter beyond Seoul: How to revive struggling provincial economies, spread the gains from export growth and prepare regions for future-oriented industries. The election also exposed an institutional weakness that should concern all democrats. Ballot shortages at multiple polling stations in Seoul disrupted voting and forced temporary suspensions. In one district, voting continued long after exit polls had been broadcast. Protesters later blocked the transfer of ballot boxes amid allegations of fraud. The National Election Commission has rejected calls for a revote, but the larger lesson remains. Administrative incompetence is fertile ground for conspiracy theories. Now comes the more difficult phase. South Korea enters a rare two-year period without a nationwide election before the 2028 parliamentary vote. In a political system consumed by permanent campaigning, such opportunities are uncommon. The government can no longer plausibly blame obstruction, gridlock or divided authority for policy failures. Korea’s potential growth continues to decline. Demographic pressures are intensifying. Wealth and opportunity are increasingly unevenly distributed. The recent boom in exports and equity markets provides relief, but cannot cure deeper structural weaknesses. The local elections stripped away many of the political excuses governments typically enjoy. That may prove their most enduring consequence. The Lee government spent its first year defining ambitions. Its second will be judged by execution. Economic polarization, slowing potential growth and labor-market rigidities will not be solved by slogans or retrospective battles. The voters who dismantled much of the conservative opposition were not endorsing permanent one-party rule. They were demanding results. Governments rarely receive a clearer opportunity. The bill for this political mortgage will come due soon enough. editorial.pullQuote: Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages.
summary: South Korea's ruling party won 12 of 16 local posts, consolidating power but not consensus.
High turnout masked poor campaigning focused on national feuds rather than regional needs.
With no elections until 2028, the government must deliver results instead of blaming obstruction.
editorial.stance: Electoral dominance demands policy delivery, not excuses; structural problems require urgent action.
summaryTr: 한국의 여당이 16개 지방직 중 12개를 차지하며 권력을 공고히 했지만 사회적 합의에는 이르지 못했다.
높은 투표율 속에서도 지역 현안보다는 국가적 갈등에 집중한 부실한 선거운동이 드러났다.
2028년까지 선거가 없는 만큼 정부는 이제 야당의 저항을 탓하기보다는 실질적 성과를 내놓아야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
지방선거에서 유권자들은 권력을 부여했지만 성과, 절제, 개혁을 요구하고 있다

선거 승리는 종종 위임이라고 불린다. 실제로는 오늘 권력을 부여하면서 나중에 정부에 청구서를 보내는 모기지처럼 기능한다. 한국의 6월 3일 지방선거는 이제 누가 국가의 정치적 미래를 소유하고 있는지 의심의 여지가 없게 만들었다. 이준석 대통령이 취임한 지 1년 만에 행정부는 국가의 상당 부분에 영향력을 확대했고, 집권 민주당은 16개 주요 시장 및 도지사 자리 중 12개를 확보했다. 이미 대통령직과 국회를 확보한 데 이어 이제는 국가의 대부분의 지방 정치 기구를 장악하고 있으며, 정치 권력의 비상한 집중을 완성했다. 그러나 선거 결과는 보기에 좋은 백지수표는 아니었다. 주요 야당인 국민의힘은 오세훈이 역사적인 5선에 당선되어 서울 시장직을 유지했고, 여러 보수 강점을 지켜냈다. 동시에 열린 국회 재보궐선거에서 집권당은 이전에 장악했던 4개 의석을 내주었다. 유권자들은 정부를 강화했지만, 또한 지배가 합의를 의미하지 않는다는 신호를 보냈다.

투표율 자체가 메시지를 전달했다. 61.0퍼센트의 투표율은 1995년 이후 지방선거에서 가장 높은 수준에 도달했다. 이러한 참여는 보통 민주주의의 활력으로 칭송된다. 하지만 유권자들을 투표소로 나가게 한 힘은 지방 정치보다는 국가적 대립에 관한 것이었다. 집권당은 선거를 전 윤석열 대통령 시기의 잔재에 대한 최종 판단으로 프레임했고, 보수진영은 일당 지배 가능성에 맞서 동원되었다. 그 결과는 매우 활기찬 유권자층이었지만, 반드시 더 숙고적인 것은 아니었다.

실제로 선거운동의 약점 중 일부는 명백했다. 선거법 위반이 급격히 증가했다. 수백 명의 후보자들이 지역 강점에서 무투표로 당선되었고, 수백만 명의 유권자들에게 의미 있는 선택 기회를 주지 못했다. 지역 발전에 대한 경쟁이 되어야 할 것이 국가 정치 전쟁의 또 다른 장이 되었다. 이 실패는 양 당에 모두 책임이 있다. 집권진영은 선거운동의 대부분을 역사적이고 사법적인 점수를 정산하는 데 집중했다. 야당은 자신의 과거에 대한 논쟁에 갇혀, 윤석열 정부의 붕괴에서 교훈을 얻었다는 것을 유권자들에게 확신시키지 못했다. 양측 모두 서울을 넘어 중요한 문제들에 충분한 관심을 기울이지 않았다: 어려움을 겪고 있는 지방 경제를 어떻게 부활시킬 것인가, 수출 성장으로 인한 이득을 어떻게 확산시킬 것인가, 지역을 미래 지향적 산업에 어떻게 준비시킬 것인가.

이번 선거는 모든 민주주의자들을 우려하게 해야 할 제도적 약점도 드러냈다. 서울의 여러 투표소에서 투표용지 부족으로 투표가 중단되고 일시적 투표 중단이 강요되었다. 한 지역에서는 출구조사가 방송된 이후까지 투표가 계속되었다. 이후 시위자들은 부정행위 의혹 속에서 투표함 이송을 저지했다. 중앙선거관리위원회는 재투표 요구를 거부했지만, 더 큰 교훈은 남아있다. 행정적 무능력은 음모론의 비옥한 토양이다.

이제 더 어려운 국면이 시작된다. 한국은 2028년 국회의원 선거 전 2년간 전국 규모의 선거가 없는 드문 시기에 진입했다. 영구적인 선거 활동에 소비되는 정치 체제에서 그러한 기회는 드물다. 정부는 더 이상 정책 실패에 대해 방해, 교착 상태 또는 권력 분산을 그럴듯하게 탓할 수 없다. 한국의 잠재 성장률은 계속 하락하고 있다. 인구 압박은 심화하고 있다. 부와 기회는 점점 더 불균등하게 분배되고 있다. 최근의 수출과 주식시장의 호황은 완화를 제공하지만 더 깊은 구조적 약점을 치료할 수는 없다. 지방선거는 정부들이 일반적으로 누리는 많은 정치적 핑계를 벗겨냈다. 그것이 가장 오래 지속될 결과일 수 있다. 이 정부는 첫 해를 야망을 정의하는 데 보냈다. 두 번째 해는 실행으로 평가될 것이다. 경제 양극화, 둔화하는 잠재 성장률, 노동시장의 경직성은 표어나 회고적 전투로는 해결되지 않을 것이다. 보수 야당의 상당 부분을 와해시킨 유권자들은 영구적인 일당 통치를 지지하지 않았다. 그들은 성과를 요구했다. 정부들은 드물게 더 명확한 기회를 받는다. 이 정치적 모기지에 대한 청구서는 곧 만기가 될 것이다.

editorials.title: Mandate without excuses
editorials.body: In local elections, voters granted power but demand results, restraint, reform Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages, granting power today while billing governments later. South Korea’s June 3 local elections have left little doubt about who now owns the country's political future. One year after taking office, President Lee Jae Myung's administration has extended its reach across much of the country, with the ruling Democratic Party of Korea capturing 12 of 16 major mayoral and gubernatorial posts. Having already secured the presidency and the National Assembly, it now commands most of the nation’s local political machinery, completing an extraordinary concentration of political power. Yet the election results were not quite the blank check they may appear to be. The main opposition People Power Party retained the Seoul mayoralty, with Oh Se-hoon winning a historic fifth term, and held on to several conservative strongholds. In concurrent parliamentary by-elections, the ruling party surrendered four seats it had previously controlled. Voters strengthened the government, but also signaled that dominance is not consensus. The turnout itself carried a message. At 61.0 percent, participation reached its highest level in a local election since 1995. Such engagement is usually hailed as democratic vitality. Yet the forces driving voters to the polls were less about local governance than national confrontation. The ruling party framed the election as a final judgment on the remnants of the period under former President Yoon Suk Yeol, while conservatives mobilized against the prospect of one-party dominance. The result was a highly energized electorate, but not necessarily a more deliberative one. Indeed, some of the campaign’s weaknesses were hard to miss. Election-law violations rose sharply. Hundreds of candidates ran unopposed in regional strongholds, leaving millions of voters without meaningful choices. What should have been a contest over regional development became another chapter in national political warfare. That failure belongs to both parties. The ruling camp spent much of the campaign focused on settling historical and judicial scores. The opposition remained trapped in arguments about its own past, unable to persuade voters that it had learned from the collapse of the Yoon presidency. Neither side devoted sufficient attention to questions that matter beyond Seoul: How to revive struggling provincial economies, spread the gains from export growth and prepare regions for future-oriented industries. The election also exposed an institutional weakness that should concern all democrats. Ballot shortages at multiple polling stations in Seoul disrupted voting and forced temporary suspensions. In one district, voting continued long after exit polls had been broadcast. Protesters later blocked the transfer of ballot boxes amid allegations of fraud. The National Election Commission has rejected calls for a revote, but the larger lesson remains. Administrative incompetence is fertile ground for conspiracy theories. Now comes the more difficult phase. South Korea enters a rare two-year period without a nationwide election before the 2028 parliamentary vote. In a political system consumed by permanent campaigning, such opportunities are uncommon. The government can no longer plausibly blame obstruction, gridlock or divided authority for policy failures. Korea’s potential growth continues to decline. Demographic pressures are intensifying. Wealth and opportunity are increasingly unevenly distributed. The recent boom in exports and equity markets provides relief, but cannot cure deeper structural weaknesses. The local elections stripped away many of the political excuses governments typically enjoy. That may prove their most enduring consequence. The Lee government spent its first year defining ambitions. Its second will be judged by execution. Economic polarization, slowing potential growth and labor-market rigidities will not be solved by slogans or retrospective battles. The voters who dismantled much of the conservative opposition were not endorsing permanent one-party rule. They were demanding results. Governments rarely receive a clearer opportunity. The bill for this political mortgage will come due soon enough. editorials.pullQuote: Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages.
summary: South Korea's ruling party won 12 of 16 local posts, consolidating power but not consensus.
High turnout masked poor campaigning focused on national feuds rather than regional needs.
With no elections until 2028, the government must deliver results instead of blaming obstruction.
editorials.stance: Electoral dominance demands policy delivery, not excuses; structural problems require urgent action.
summaryTr: 한국의 여당이 16개 지방직 중 12개를 차지하며 권력을 공고히 했지만 사회적 합의에는 이르지 못했다.
높은 투표율 속에서도 지역 현안보다는 국가적 갈등에 집중한 부실한 선거운동이 드러났다.
2028년까지 선거가 없는 만큼 정부는 이제 야당의 저항을 탓하기보다는 실질적 성과를 내놓아야 한다.

editorial.body: 오세훈 국민의힘 후보가 3일 치러진 서울시장 선거에서 막판 역전승을 거뒀다. 국민의힘에서 제명된 한동훈 무소속 후보도 부산 북갑 보궐선거에서 승리하며 국회 입성에 성공했다. 두 사람은 이번 선거 기간 동안 ‘윤 어게인’ 세력에 포획된 장동혁 국민의힘 대표와 철저히 거리

editorial.titleTr: [Opinion] Yoon Suk-yeol and Han Dong-hoon's Victory Reaffirms Case for Complete Party Overhaul
summaryTr: Oh Se-hun achieves come-from-behind victory in Seoul mayoral election
Han Dong-hun, independent candidate, enters National Assembly with victory in Busan North District by-election
Two figures reaffirm necessity of reforming ruling party
editorial.bodyTr: Oh Se-hun, the People Power Party candidate, pulled off a dramatic last-minute comeback victory in the Seoul mayoral election held on the 3rd. Han Dong-hun, an independent candidate expelled from the People Power Party, also won in the Busan North Gap by-election and successfully secured a seat in the National Assembly. During the election period, the two individuals maintained a clear distance from People Power Party Chairman Jang Dong-hyuk, who has been captured by the "Yoon Again" faction.

editorials.title: [사설] 북 새 농축시설 공개, 우려되는 핵능력 강화
editorials.body: 김정은 북한 국무위원장이 “새로 조업한 핵물질 생산공장을 현지 지도”했다고 북한 관영 언론이 전했다. 북한은 김 위원장이 고농축(HEU) 우라늄 제조시설을 뜻하는 ‘무기급 핵물질 생산기지’를 시찰했다는 사실을 밝힌 적은 있지만, 지금까지 가동되지 않던 새 시설을 방문했

summaryTr: Kim Jong Un inspects new nuclear material production facility
Highly enriched uranium manufacturing facility begins operation
Concerns deepen over North Korea's strengthened nuclear capabilities
editorials.bodyTr: North Korea's state media reported that leader Kim Jong Un conducted an on-site inspection of a newly operational nuclear material production facility. While North Korea has previously acknowledged that Kim visited a "weapons-grade nuclear material production base," referring to a highly enriched uranium facility, this marks the first time he has visited a new facility that had not been in operation until now.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Democrats' Seoul Loss Demands Soul-Searching and a Return to the People's Will
summaryTr: The Democratic Party suffered defeat to the People Power Party in the Seoul mayoral election.
Candidate Jung Won-o was overtaken by candidate Oh Se-hoon during the vote counting process.
He tasted the bitter cup of defeat with a margin of approximately 53,000 votes.
editorials.bodyTr: The Democratic Party of Korea suffered a crushing defeat to the People Power Party in the Seoul mayoral election, which it had expected to win easily. Democratic Party candidate Jung Won-o, who had consistently led in pre-election polls, was ahead throughout the ballot counting after votes were opened on the evening of the 3rd, but was overtaken by People Power Party candidate Oh Se-hoon in the early morning of the 4th after the counting rate exceeded 90 percent. The margin was approximately 53,000 votes.

summaryTr: Oh Se-hun, People Power Party candidate, stages dramatic last-minute comeback victory in Seoul mayoral election.
Historic fifth-term Seoul mayor elected as exit polls show reversal beyond margin of error.
Government and ruling party must take seriously the people's will to check unchecked dominance.
editorial.bodyTr: In the June 3 local elections, Oh Se-hun, the ruling People Power Party candidate, won the Seoul mayoral race, the most hotly contested battle of the election, becoming the first five-term Seoul mayor in history. editorials.titleTr: Electoral Commission Must Severely Punish Officials and Undertake Fundamental Reform at Organizational Level
summaryTr: A shortage of ballots occurred at 14 polling stations during the June 3 local elections.
An unprecedented crisis of transporting ballots in ziplock bags exposed lax election management.
The election commission says those responsible must face strict punishment and fundamental reform is needed, possibly including dissolution.
editorials.bodyTr: The unprecedented ballot paper shortage that occurred during the June 3 local elections left an indelible stain on the electoral history of the Republic of Korea. Voters were unable to cast their ballots at fourteen polling stations in areas including Gangnam, Songpa, and Gwangjin in Seoul due to depleted supplies, and the later scene of transporting ballots in ziplock bags starkly exposed the current state of election management. This incident, which violated citizens' voting rights and provided grounds for challenging election results, represents a grave failure of the National Election Commission's management capabilities.

summaryTr: The won-dollar exchange rate has risen to the 1,540 won level.
Concerns about side effects are growing to a level rarely seen since the financial crisis.

editorials.bodyTr: The won-dollar exchange rate has surged to around 1,540 won per dollar. editorial.pullQuote: '절묘한 견제'를 선택한 민심…여야 모두 겸허하게 새겨야
summary: 국민은 여야 어느 한쪽을 절대 지지하지 않는 '절묘한 견제'를 선택했다.
이는 권력 집중을 경계하고 균형잡힌 정치를 원하는 민심의 표현이다.
여당과 야당 모두 겸허함으로 이 결과를 받아들여야 한다.
editorial.stance: 국민의 균형잡힌 정치 선택을 존중하며 여야가 책임감 있게 응해야 한다는 입장
editorial.titleTr: Public Mandate for Delicate Balance: Both Parties Must Heed the Message
summaryTr: The electorate has chosen a delicate check, refusing to give absolute support to either the ruling or opposition party.
This reflects the people's desire to guard against concentrated power and pursue balanced politics.
Both the ruling and opposition parties must humbly accept this result.
